nodes.label: AI as debugging assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for complex or unfamiliar code
nodes.grounding_span: I use it for all kinds of tasks, mostly coding, like debugging problems which seem unclear, or automating tedious tasks

nodes.label: AI as last resort for design tasks
nodes.grounding_span: This kind of tasks where I prefer to do it myself are more dealing with the design or architecture

nodes.label: AI code quality and reliability
nodes.grounding_span: any errors on chatbot side would introduce a lot of unfamiliar code which I then have to digest

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.grounding_span: It's more like a back and forth, because it's not rare that chatbot provides a solution that is perfect

nodes.label: AI adoption in workplace
nodes.label_negative: Balanced AI adoption with human oversight
nodes.grounding_span: since leadership pushes to use them even more, we may end up with a lot of generated code no human has verified thoroughly

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unnecessary overhead for automation
nodes.grounding_span: I think, since now I google less now, and not check random conversations of people which may or may not be relevant for my search, I am coming to the solutions much faster

nodes.label: Productivity and efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: I am coming to the solutions much faster, in more direct approach

nodes.label: Code quality and correctness
nodes.grounding_span: any new code needs to be tested, and if I ask for a bigger feature, it means I will have to deal with a large piece of unfamiliar code

nodes.label: Job security and skill relevance
nodes.grounding_span: I don't feel comfortable because in this capitalist world you are out of the window as soon as something or somebody cheaper can substitute you

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: I was surprised that it did everything just right, with only few minor details that needed change

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's inability to maintain context
nodes.grounding_span: it was filling the fields in some random order. More frustratingly, the code was full or unnecessary if else conditions

nodes.label: Concern about job displacement
nodes.grounding_span: I don't feel comfortable because in this capitalist world you are out of the window as soon as something or somebody cheaper can substitute you

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI adoption
nodes.grounding_span: I think there so much emphasis on efficiency and cost cutting these days, that we may be just blindly rushing into the unknown

nodes.label: Iterative step-by-step problem decomposition
nodes.grounding_span: it's just to big to effectively discuss all possible points at once, and that's why I break it down

nodes.label: Scope management — keeps AI tasks small to maintain control
nodes.grounding_span: That's why I keep the scope smaller, so it's more manageable for me
